metadata.name: All Spanish Words
metadata.description: First-generation Spanish vocabulary entries in canonical dictionary form, with English glosses and lightweight grammatical metadata.

schema.label: Lemma
schema.description: Canonical dictionary form. Use the infinitive for verbs, singular masculine/base form for adjectives where applicable, and singular noun form.

schema.label: Meaning
schema.description: Short English gloss. Keep it compact; put nuance in notes later if needed.

values.proper-noun: Name of a person, place, organization, etc.

schema.label: Semantic Class
schema.description: Semantic category/class of the word. Use plain text rather than an enum so this can scale across parts of speech; examples for nouns include home, food, animals, people, city, and time. Use null when not needed.

schema.label: Gender
schema.description: Gender classification for nouns; use null when not applicable.

values.mf: Common gender / masculine and feminine forms.
values.f_el: Feminine noun that takes singular `el` before stressed /a/ (for example, `el agua`, `el águila`).

entries.meaning: to be located; to find oneself
to tidy; to put in order
to watch; to keep an eye on
